title: Lecanthus peduncularis
taxonomy: Lecanthus peduncularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The plant belongs to the genus Lecanthus, which is part of the larger family Acanthaceae. The Acanthaceae family consists of flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or acanthus family. Lecanthus peduncularis is typically classified as a perennial plant.

Lecanoraceae is a family of lichen-forming fungi. The Lecanoraceae family, including Lecanora, has been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and infections.

The bioactive constituents of Lecanoraceae include phenolic compounds, such as depsides and depsidones, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

The most common herbal preparations made with Lecanoraceae include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health and well-being.
